description: Rules for linting .mcph.json — the config file read by the @yawlabs/mcph CLI (which orchestrates MCP servers via the mcp.hosting registry). Distinct from .mcp.json (client-side server list) — different threat model, different schema. Applies across the user-global (~/.mcph.json), per-project (.mcph.json), and machine-local (.mcph.local.json) scope cascade.

categories.name: Token Security
categories.description: Detects PAT (mcp_pat_*) leaks, format errors, and promotes env-var-only posture.

categories.name: API Endpoint Validation
categories.description: Validates the apiBase URL, flags plaintext HTTP to public hosts.

categories.name: Schema Conformance
categories.description: Catches unknown/typo'd fields and stale schema versions.

categories.name: Allow/Deny List Semantics
categories.description: Flags conflicts and duplicates in the servers/blocked arrays.

categories.name: Gitignore Hygiene
categories.description: Ensures machine-local override files never get committed.

rules.description: A project-scope .mcph.json that is git-tracked contains a "token" field. The PAT will leak via git history.
rules.message: "token" in a git-tracked project-scope .mcph.json — PAT will leak via git history
rules.remediation: Move the token to ~/.mcph.json, .mcph.local.json (gitignored), or export MCPH_TOKEN as an environment variable. Rotate the token immediately if already committed.

rules.description: A .mcph.local.json file exists but is not covered by .gitignore. The file exists precisely so machine-local overrides stay machine-local.
rules.message: {file} is not covered by .gitignore — machine-local overrides can leak via git
rules.fixDescription: Append ".mcph.local.json" to .gitignore in the project root.

rules.description: The "token" value doesn't match the mcp.hosting PAT format (^mcp_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$).
rules.trigger: token present and regex match fails

rules.description: The "apiBase" URL uses plaintext HTTP to a public (non-localhost, non-private) host. The MCPH_TOKEN would travel in the clear.
rules.message: "apiBase" uses plaintext HTTP to a public host ({hostname})
rules.remediation: Use https:// instead. Private hosts (localhost, 10.*, 192.168.*, 172.16–31.*, *.local, *.internal) are allowed without TLS.

rules.description: The "apiBase" field is not a valid absolute URL.
rules.remediation: Use an absolute http(s) URL, e.g. "https://mcp.hosting".

rules.description: A top-level field appears that is not in the mcph config schema. Usually a typo (tokens vs token, blockList vs blocked).
rules.trigger: top-level key not in { $schema, version, token, apiBase, servers, blocked }
rules.message: unknown field "{name}" — not in the mcph config schema

rules.description: The "version" field is older than the current schema version.
rules.message: "version": {value} is older than the current schema version ({current})
rules.remediation: Bump to the current version. Older values continue to load but may miss newer fields.

rules.description: A server namespace appears in both the allow-list ("servers") and deny-list ("blocked"). Deny wins in practice, so the allow-list entry is dead weight.
rules.remediation: Remove the namespace from one of the two lists.

rules.description: The same value appears more than once in a servers or blocked array.
rules.trigger: duplicate string in servers[] or blocked[]
rules.message: "{value}" appears multiple times in "{listName}" (first at line {firstLine})

rules.description: The "token" field is present in any .mcph.json scope. An environment variable (MCPH_TOKEN) is safer — files survive backups, screen shares, cloud sync, and editor indexing.
rules.trigger: token field present in any scope (and token-in-project-scope did not already fire)
rules.message: prefer MCPH_TOKEN env var over a file-stored token in {file}
rules.remediation: Delete the "token" field and export MCPH_TOKEN in your shell/env manager (bash/zsh, fish, PowerShell, direnv).
